Who is speaking in the poem?A teacher 	(b) A mother 	(c) A friend 	(d) A sisterWhat does the phrase “crystal stair” suggest?A magical place  (b) A life of luxury and ease (c) A royal palace (d) A staircase of glass  3. What does the mother continue doing despite hardships? 	(a) Sleeping 	(b) Complaining 	(c) Running away 	(d) Climbing 	4. How does the mother describe parts of her journey? 	(a) Bright and beautiful   	(b) Easy and comfortable   	(c) Dark and difficult 	  	(d) Short and simpleComplete the following lines 	Critical ThinkingIt’s had tacks in it, 	2. _________________________ 	 _________________________ 	 And reachin’ landin’s, 	 _________________________ 	 And turnin’ corners, 	 And places with no carpet on the floor— 	 ___________________________ 	 Bare. 	 ___________________________Answer the following questions with reference to the context.“Life for me ain’t been no crystal stair.”Name the poem and the poet.Who speaks these lines and to whom?What does the speaker want to convey through this line? 	2. “Don’t you set down on the steps  	 ’Cause you finds it’s kinder hard.  	 a.  What advice is the mother giving?What do “steps” represent?Why should he not “set down”?	Dictionary & Vocabulary Skills	  	/Look up the word ‘step’ in a dictionary. Write two different meanings of the word.__________________________________________________________________________________________________________________________________________  Now identify the meaning used in each sentence.   a.  She took a step towards the door. ________Success comes one step at a time. ________The staircase had twenty steps. ________A metaphor is a figure of speech that involves an implied comparison between two unlike things without using ‘like’, ‘as’, or ‘as…as’. It suggests that one thing is another, highlighting similarities between them to add deeper meaning or understanding.“Life for me ain’t been no crystal stair.”Here, life is compared to a staircase.Write ‘M’ for metaphor and ‘S’ for simile. 	(i) Her mind was a storm of thoughts. ________________________ 	(ii) He fought like a lion in the battle.  ________________________ 	(iii) Time is a thief that steals our moments.  ________________________ 	(iv) The classroom was as noisy as a market.   ________________________ 	(v) His words were daggers that hurt deeply.   ________________________ 	(vi) She shone like a star on the stage.   ________________________Scan & LearnScan the QR code to access Ask questions, clear yout the worksheet for this doubts, and learn more with chapter.AI.
